title: DaemonSet — Communicating with Daemon Pods
text:
Some possible patterns for communicating with Pods in a DaemonSet are:
Push
: Pods in the DaemonSet are configured to send updates to another service, such
as a stats database. They do not have clients.
NodeIP and Known Port
: Pods in the DaemonSet can use a
hostPort
, so that the pods
are reachable via the node IPs.
Clients know the list of node IPs somehow, and know the port by convention.
DNS
: Create a
headless service
with the same pod selector, and then discover DaemonSets using the
endpoints
resource or retrieve multiple A records from DNS.
Service
: Create a service with the same Pod selector, and use the service to reach a
daemon on a random node. Use
Service Internal Traffic Policy
to limit to pods on the same node.
